task_completion.description: Verify the agent queries the correct target index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Splunk search bar or report configuration containing 'index="system_logs"' or 'index=system_logs'.

task_completion.description: Verify the search analyzes log volume over time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for time-series commands in the search string (e.g., 'timechart count', 'stats count by _time') or a visual time-series graph (line/bar chart over time) in the search results.

task_completion.description: Verify a new report has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a newly created report in the Splunk 'Reports' tab, or a 'Save As Report' confirmation dialog.

task_completion.description: Verify the saved report is configured to run on a schedule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Schedule' column in the Reports list showing an active schedule (e.g., a cron expression or schedule description), or the 'Edit Schedule' dialog with the 'Schedule Report' checkbox enabled and saved.

task_completion.description: Verify an operational dashboard was created with panels showing log volume trends.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk 'Dashboards' view showing a custom dashboard that contains at least one panel displaying a chart or graph of log volume over time.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Splunk Web UI to create the reports and dashboards.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Splunk web interface in a browser (http://localhost:8000), rather than terminal windows modifying Splunk .conf files directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with actual log data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The search results, report previews, or dashboard panels should display actual data points, events, or graphs, not just empty mockups or static HTML edits.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the UI using automated scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows running curl commands to the Splunk REST API or python scripts designed to generate the dashboard/report automatically.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target Splunk index must be exactly 'system_logs'. The scoring breakdown requires 5 specific criteria worth 20 points each: 1) a new report is created, 2) the report references 'system_logs', 3) the report uses time analysis (e.g., timechart), 4) the report is scheduled, and 5) a dashboard with panels is created.
